proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen and patients not receiving treatment_tamoxifen.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_palbociclib and patients not receiving treatment_palbociclib.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab and patients not receiving treatment_trastuzumab.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_olaparib and patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan and patients not receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab and patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.723 on tamoxifen vs 4.680 off (t-test).
Mean PFS 5.408 on palbociclib vs 4.310 off; large positive crude effect.
Mean PFS 4.645 on trastuzumab vs 4.705 off; small negative crude effect.
Mean PFS 4.660 on olaparib vs 4.696 off; not significant overall.
Mean PFS 4.686 on sacituzumab vs 4.694 off; null.
Mean PFS 4.630 on pembrolizumab vs 4.704 off; small negative crude effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (continuous).
Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive correlation between age and PFS (r=+0.70).
Higher Ki-67 associated with worse PFS (r=-0.091).
No correlation between tumor size and PFS.
Higher albumin associated with longer PFS (r=+0.10).
Higher LDH weakly associated with shorter PFS (r=-0.016).
More weight loss associated with shorter PFS (r=-0.116).
No association between CRP and PFS.
No association between NLR and PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with stage_iv=0.
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without brain metastases.
Patients with er_positive=1 have longer pfs_months than ER-negative patients.
Patients with her2_positive=1 have shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative patients.
Patients with pik3ca_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than wild-type.
Patients with brca1_mutation=1 differ in pfs_months versus wild-type.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 differ in pfs_months versus wild-type.
ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is inversely associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Stage IV: mean PFS 3.62 vs 5.15 in non-stage IV; large negative effect.
Brain mets: mean PFS 3.75 vs 4.80; large negative effect.
ER+ patients have higher mean PFS (4.86 vs 4.30).
HER2+ patients have lower mean PFS (4.32 vs 4.78).
PIK3CA mutated patients have lower mean PFS (4.33 vs 4.89).
BRCA1: trivial difference in PFS.
BRCA2: weak negative trend in PFS, NS.
ECOG 0/1/2 mean PFS = 5.64/4.44/3.29; strong inverse relationship (r=-0.32).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In an OLS model adjusting for all baseline features, treatment_palbociclib has a positive independent main effect on pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_tamoxifen has no main effect on pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_trastuzumab has no positive main effect on pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_olaparib has no main effect on pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has no main effect on pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab has no main effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, age_years remains positively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib coefficient +1.10 months, p<1e-300 in fully-adjusted multivariable model.
Tamoxifen coefficient -0.008, NS in adjusted model.
Trastuzumab coefficient -0.009, NS in adjusted model.
Olaparib coefficient -0.026, NS in adjusted model.
Sacituzumab coefficient +0.033, borderline (p=0.049).
Pembrolizumab coefficient -0.020, NS in adjusted model.
Age coefficient +0.176/yr (large), p<1e-300; older patients have substantially longer PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_tamoxifen interacts with er_positive: the tamoxifen effect on pfs_months is more positive in ER-positive patients than ER-negative.
treatment_palbociclib interacts with er_positive: the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is more positive in ER-positive patients than ER-negative.
treatment_palbociclib interacts with hr_pos (er_positive OR pr_positive): the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in HR-positive patients.
treatment_trastuzumab interacts with her2_positive: the trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is more positive in HER2-positive patients than HER2-negative.

analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen × ER interaction coefficient +0.0073, NS.
Palbociclib × ER interaction +1.56 months, p≈0; very strong qualitative interaction.
Palbociclib × HR-positive interaction +1.46 months, p≈0.
Trastuzumab × HER2+ interaction +0.009, NS. Trastuzumab does not appear to have a HER2-specific PFS benefit in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib interacts with brca1_mutation OR brca2_mutation (brca_any): the olaparib effect on pfs_months is more positive in BRCA-mutant patients.
treatment_olaparib has a stronger positive effect on pfs_months in patients with brca1_mutation specifically.
treatment_olaparib has a stronger positive effect on pfs_months in patients with brca2_mutation specifically.
treatment_pembrolizumab interacts with tnbc (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0): pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is more positive in TNBC patients.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan interacts with tnbc: sacituzumab effect on pfs_months is more positive in TNBC patients.

analyses.result_summary: Olaparib × BRCA-any interaction +0.045 months, NS.
Olaparib × BRCA1 interaction +0.10, NS.
Olaparib × BRCA2 interaction -0.016, NS.
Pembrolizumab × TNBC interaction +0.067, borderline (p=0.086).
Sacituzumab × TNBC interaction -0.018, NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib has a strongly negative interaction with pik3ca_mutation: palbociclib's positive effect on pfs_months is suppressed in PIK3CA-mutant tumors.
treatment_palbociclib has a strongly negative interaction with her2_positive: palbociclib's positive effect on pfs_months is suppressed in HER2-positive tumors.
treatment_palbociclib has a positive interaction with pr_positive on pfs_months.
treatment_palbociclib has a negative interaction with tnbc on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Palbo × PIK3CA-mut interaction = -1.665, p≈0; in PIK3CA-mut tumors, palbociclib has no benefit.
Palbo × HER2+ interaction = -1.338, p≈0; HER2+ patients do not benefit from palbociclib.
Palbo × PR+ interaction = +1.030, p≈0.
Palbo × TNBC interaction = -1.380, p≈0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among ER-positive patients, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to ER-positive patients not on palbociclib.
Among ER-negative patients, treatment_palbociclib has no effect on pfs_months.
Among HR-positive (er_positive=1 OR pr_positive=1) patients, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months.
Among HR-positive HER2-negative patients, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months.
Among HR-positive HER2-negative PIK3CA wild-type patients, treatment_palbociclib has the largest increase in mean pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: ER+ subgroup (n=34886): palbo on=12194 mean=5.890, off=22692 mean=4.313, diff=+1.577.
ER- subgroup (n=15114): palbo on=5234 mean=4.285, off=9880 mean=4.303, diff=-0.017.
HR+ subgroup (n=37166): palbo on=12968 mean=5.792, off=24198 mean=4.308, diff=+1.484.
HR+/HER2- subgroup (n=30444): palbo on=10598 mean=6.119, off=19846 mean=4.308, diff=+1.811.
HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt subgroup (n=19918): palbo on=6987 mean=7.038, off=12931 mean=4.297, diff=+2.740.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among HER2-positive patients, treatment_trastuzumab increases mean pfs_months relative to HER2+ patients not on trastuzumab.
Among HER2-positive node-positive patients, treatment_trastuzumab increases mean pfs_months.
Among HER2-low patients, treatment_trastuzumab increases mean pfs_months (a HER2-low expansion analogue).
Among HER2-negative patients, treatment_trastuzumab has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: HER2+ subgroup (n=9060): trastuzumab on=1890 mean=4.254, off=7170 mean=4.334, diff=-0.079.
HER2+/node+ subgroup (n=4480): trastuzumab diff=-0.095.
HER2-low subgroup (n=18543): trastuzumab diff=-0.016.
HER2- subgroup: trastuzumab diff=-0.051, p=0.106; no benefit, slight negative trend likely chance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among BRCA1- or BRCA2-mutant patients, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months.
Among BRCA1-mutant patients specifically, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months.
Among BRCA-mutant TNBC patients, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months.
Among non-BRCA-mutant patients, treatment_olaparib has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA-any subgroup (n=2499): olaparib on=239 mean=4.933, off=2260 mean=4.587, crude diff=+0.346, p=0.04.
BRCA1+ subgroup (n=1277): olaparib crude diff=+0.398, p=0.090; trend.
BRCA+/TNBC subgroup (n=498): olaparib on=35 mean=4.901, off=463 mean=4.150, crude diff=+0.751, p=0.047.
Non-BRCA: olaparib diff=-0.056, NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases mean pfs_months relative to TNBC patients not on pembrolizumab.
Among premenopausal (postmenopausal=0) TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases mean pfs_months relative to other premenopausal TNBC patients.
Among postmenopausal TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not affect pfs_months.
Among non-TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not affect pfs_months (or has a small negative crude trend).

analyses.result_summary: TNBC subgroup (n=10496): pembrolizumab on=1578 mean=4.366, off=8918 mean=4.294, crude diff=+0.072.
Premenopausal TNBC (n=4163): pembro on=611 mean=4.502, off=3552 mean=4.227, crude diff=+0.275, p=0.003.
Postmenopausal TNBC: pembro diff=-0.059, NS.
Non-TNBC: pembrolizumab small negative crude trend, p<0.05 in some sub-strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among TNBC patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases mean pfs_months.
Among TNBC stage IV patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan affects pfs_months.
Among premenopausal TNBC patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan affects pfs_months in opposite direction to pembrolizumab.
Among HER2-low patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases mean pfs_months (HER2-low expansion analogue).

analyses.result_summary: TNBC: sacituzumab diff=-0.085, NS.
TNBC/stage IV (n=3140): sacituzumab on=333 mean=3.013, off=2807 mean=3.275, crude diff=-0.262, p=0.02.
Premenopausal TNBC: sacituzumab on=425 mean=4.016, off=3738 mean=4.295, diff=-0.279, p=0.009; opposite sign to pembrolizumab in same subgroup.
HER2-low: sacituzumab diff=-0.031, NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic screen of every treatment × binary feature interaction will reveal that the largest signed interaction is treatment_palbociclib × er_positive (positive).
The systematic screen will identify treatment_palbociclib × pik3ca_mutation as the largest negative interaction (palbociclib loses efficacy in PIK3CA-mutant tumors).
The screen will reveal no other treatment with an interaction p-value below 1e-50, indicating only palbociclib has strong qualitative subgroup heterogeneity.

analyses.code: for tx in treatments: for b in binaries: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {tx}*{b} + covars').fit()
analyses.result_summary: Top six interactions all involve palbociclib: palbo×er_positive +1.56 (p~0), palbo×hr_pos +1.46 (p~0), palbo×pr_positive +1.03 (p~0), palbo×pik3ca_mutation -1.67 (p~0), palbo×her2_positive -1.34 (p~0), palbo×tnbc -1.38 (p~0). No other treatment has an interaction with p<1e-50.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib × ki67_pct interaction is negative on pfs_months: palbociclib benefit declines with higher Ki-67.
treatment_palbociclib × age_years interaction on pfs_months is small/null.
Among continuous variables × treatment interactions screened, treatment_palbociclib × ki67_pct will have the strongest signal.

analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib × ki67_pct interaction = -0.053 per % Ki-67, p<1e-100. Strongest cont. interaction.
Palbociclib × age interaction -0.0016/yr, NS (p=0.13).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt patients, treatment_palbociclib roughly doubles mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
After full multivariable adjustment, the palbociclib effect within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt remains essentially unchanged from the unadjusted estimate.
Outside HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt, palbociclib has no detectable effect on pfs_months (effect estimate near zero).

analyses.result_summary: ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt subgroup (n=18681): palbo mean PFS 7.21 vs 4.30 off; crude diff +2.908, p~0.
Adjusted palbociclib coefficient in target subgroup: +2.920, p~0; very stable.
Outside target subgroup (n=31319): adjusted palbociclib coefficient +0.0004, p=0.87.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment, treatment_olaparib has no main effect on pfs_months in the BRCA1-mutant subgroup, indicating apparent crude effect is confounded.
After adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab in the premenopausal TNBC subgroup is essentially zero, contradicting the larger crude effect.
After adjustment, treatment_trastuzumab in HER2-positive patients has no detectable effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan in TNBC patients has no detectable effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, treatment_tamoxifen in ER-positive patients has no detectable effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA1+ subgroup adjusted olaparib coef +0.084, p=0.46. Crude +0.40 attenuates with adjustment.
Premenop TNBC adjusted pembrolizumab coef +0.015, p=0.05; crude +0.275 attenuates fully (driven by age/stage imbalance).
HER2+ adjusted trastuzumab coef -0.0002, p=0.95.
TNBC adjusted sacituzumab coef +0.006, p=0.32.
ER+ adjusted tamoxifen coef approximately 0; no benefit detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Exhaustive 2-feature subgroup enumeration will show that the strongest positive subgroup for treatment_palbociclib is er_positive=1 AND pik3ca_mutation=0, with mean PFS difference >2 months.
Exhaustive 2-feature subgroup enumeration will fail to reveal any other treatment with a 2-feature subgroup PFS difference exceeding +0.5 months that survives multivariable adjustment.
Exhaustive 2-feature subgroup enumeration will show several subgroups where treatment_trastuzumab is associated with shorter pfs_months (likely chance, but with consistent slight negative direction).

analyses.code: Brute-force enumeration over all (binary feature, value) pairs of size 2; min n=50 per arm; t-test
analyses.result_summary: Top 2-feature subgroup for palbociclib: er_positive=1 & pik3ca_mutation=0 (n=22788), palbo on=8034 mean=6.680, off=14754 mean=4.305, diff=+2.376, p~0. Multiple ER+/PIK3CA-wt-adjacent subgroups follow with diff +1.8 to +2.4.

No other treatment achieves an unadjusted 2-feature subgroup |diff|>0.5 that also survives covariate adjustment. Top non-palbo signals: olaparib in male/BRCA1+ +0.83 (p=0.01, but small n=65 vs 631), trastuzumab in postmenop/PR- -0.16, sacituzumab/pembro premenop/TNBC ±0.28 (opposite directions).

Trastuzumab top 2-feature subgroups all show small negative crude effects (-0.12 to -0.16, n=2000-15000). Direction is consistently negative across many strata, but adjusted models show effects collapse to ~0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Exhaustive 3-feature subgroup enumeration will show that ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-wt is the optimal subgroup for treatment_palbociclib, with diff approaching +3 months.
Exhaustive 3-feature enumeration will show treatment_olaparib has no 3-feature subgroup with adjusted diff > 0.3 months that is biologically interpretable.

Exhaustive 3-feature enumeration will show treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in postmenopausal=0 AND tnbc=1 only (and similar HR-/premenopausal definitions), but the magnitude is small (<0.3 crude, near 0 adjusted).

analyses.result_summary: Top 3-feature subgroup for palbociclib: er_positive=1 & her2_positive=0 & pik3ca_mutation=0 (n=18681, palbo on=6571, mean 7.21 vs 4.30, diff +2.908, p~0). Equivalent: er_positive=1 & her2_low=1 & pik3ca_mutation=0 (n=8518, diff +2.915).

Top olaparib 3-feature: sex_female=0 & node_positive=1 & brca1_mutation=1 (n=355; on=34 mean=6.00, off=321 mean=4.39; crude diff +1.60, p=0.0006). Adjusted analysis attenuates to near-null. Likely chance/confounding.
Pembrolizumab top subgroups all = postmenopausal=0 & tnbc=1 (and equivalent HR-/ER-/PR-/HER2- variants), crude diff +0.275, p=0.003. Adjusted residual ~+0.015.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the palbociclib target subgroup ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt, the palbociclib effect is similar regardless of HER2-low status (no further heterogeneity).
Within the palbociclib target subgroup, the palbociclib effect is similar regardless of BRCA mutation status.
PR positivity does not further refine the palbociclib subgroup beyond ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt: ER+/PR-/HER2-/PIK3CAwt patients still benefit, while ER-/PR+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt do not.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind stratified by her2_low within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt
analyses.result_summary: ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt/her2_low=0: palbo diff +2.903; her2_low=1: +2.915. Indistinguishable.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind stratified by brca_any within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt
analyses.result_summary: ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt/brca=0: palbo diff +2.926; brca=1 (n=929): +2.552. Both very large.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind stratified by ER x PR within HER2-/PIK3CAwt
analyses.result_summary: Within HER2-/PIK3CAwt: ER+/PR- diff +2.69 (n=2765), ER+/PR+ diff +2.95 (n=15916), ER-/PR+ diff +0.09 (n=1237, NS), ER-/PR- diff -0.07 (n=6842, NS). ER positivity is the operative modifier; PR alone insufficient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: 3-way interaction trastuzumab × her2_positive × any other binary feature is null (no subgroup of HER2+ patients benefits from trastuzumab).
3-way interaction pembrolizumab × tnbc × any other binary feature is null after adjustment.
3-way interaction olaparib × brca_any × hr_pos (or × tnbc) is null.

analyses.code: Loop over binary mods: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_trastuzumab*her2_positive*MOD + covars').fit()
analyses.result_summary: All trast×HER2+×binary triple interactions p>0.2; no subgroup of HER2+ patients shows trastuzumab benefit.

analyses.code: Loop over binary mods: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab*tnbc*MOD + covars').fit()
analyses.result_summary: All pembro×TNBC×binary triple interactions p>0.4 after adjustment.
Olap × BRCA × TNBC triple interaction p=0.91; × HR+ p=0.90. No 3-way structure.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within TNBC, treatment_pembrolizumab × age_years interaction is negative (pembro favors younger TNBC patients).
Within TNBC, treatment_pembrolizumab × weight_loss_pct_6mo interaction is positive (more cachectic TNBC patients gain less from pembrolizumab).
Within HER2+, treatment_trastuzumab has no significant interaction with any continuous biomarker.
Within BRCA+, treatment_olaparib has no significant interaction with any continuous biomarker.
Within ER+, treatment_tamoxifen has no significant interaction with any continuous biomarker.

analyses.result_summary: Pembro×age interaction in TNBC: -0.0013/yr, p=0.013. Younger TNBC patients trend toward more pembro benefit.
Pembro×weight_loss in TNBC: +0.0047/%, p=0.0007 (positive, contrary to hypothesis); but tertile-stratified analysis showed inconsistent pattern (no monotonic dose-response).

analyses.code: Loop continuous × treatment_trastuzumab in HER2+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Only marginal trastuzumab×age in HER2+ (-0.0011/yr, p=0.038); not robust to multiple-comparison correction.

analyses.code: Loop continuous × treatment_olaparib in BRCA+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Only olaparib×bun_mg_dl in BRCA+: -0.05, p=0.003. Likely false positive given many tests.

analyses.code: Loop continuous × treatment_tamoxifen in ER+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: No significant tamoxifen×continuous interactions in ER+ subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatments are well-balanced across baseline confounders (no strong allocation bias).
Subgroup-specific differences in apparent treatment effect estimates between unadjusted and adjusted models reflect within-subgroup confounding by age (which has the largest effect on pfs_months).
Treatments are also balanced across the palbociclib target subgroup membership, so the palbociclib effect estimate is unbiased.

analyses.code: Compare means of confounders (age, ECOG, stage, ER+, HER2+, etc.) by treatment
analyses.result_summary: Mean age differs by ≤0.22 years across treatments; ECOG differs by ≤0.02; stage_iv by ≤0.01; biomarker prevalences differ by ≤0.013. Balance is excellent overall.

analyses.code: Recompute selected subgroup effects with vs without age adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Within TNBC/premenop, pembrolizumab arm patients are 0.7 yr older and 4.8% less stage IV than control; given age coef +0.176/yr, this fully explains the +0.275 crude effect.

analyses.code: Compare confounders within palbo target subgroup by palbociclib status
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt: palbociclib arms differ by ≤0.05 yr in age, ≤0.01 in ECOG, near-zero in stage, brain mets, albumin. The +2.92 effect is not attributable to confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib has a small negative residual interaction with brca_any: in BRCA-mutant patients, palbociclib gain is slightly attenuated.
treatment_pembrolizumab has a residual negative interaction with er_positive on pfs_months (pembro slightly worse in ER+ patients).
Across all 6 treatments, only treatment_palbociclib has any residual qualitative interaction with the palbo target subgroup definition (cross-treatment specificity check).

analyses.result_summary: Palbo × brca_any interaction = -0.127, p=0.009. In BRCA+, palbociclib effect is slightly smaller (still strongly positive overall).
Pembro × er_positive = -0.073, p=0.030. Modest negative residual interaction.

analyses.code: Loop each treatment × palbo_target binary indicator
analyses.result_summary: Only treatment_palbociclib has a strong palbo_target interaction (+2.91, p~0). For other treatments, the palbo_target interaction is small (|β|<0.09): tamoxifen 0.00, trastuzumab -0.02, olaparib -0.07 (p=0.06), sacituzumab +0.06 (p=0.10), pembrolizumab -0.08 (p=0.008).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final hypothesis: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months in the subgroup defined by er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0, by approximately +2.9 months relative to identical patients not on palbociclib; the unfavorable values that suppress this effect are her2_positive=1 and pik3ca_mutation=1.

Final hypothesis: treatment_trastuzumab has no measurable PFS benefit in this cohort, including in the canonical HER2-positive subgroup; a slight negative crude effect appears that does not survive adjustment.
Final hypothesis: treatment_olaparib has no measurable PFS benefit in the BRCA1- or BRCA2-mutant subgroup after multivariable adjustment.

Final hypothesis: treatment_pembrolizumab has no clinically meaningful PFS benefit; the apparent positive effect in premenopausal TNBC is fully explained by baseline imbalance (mainly age and stage IV).
Final hypothesis: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has no measurable PFS benefit in any subgroup, including TNBC and HER2-low.
Final hypothesis: treatment_tamoxifen has no measurable PFS benefit in any subgroup, including ER-positive postmenopausal patients.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt; sign-checked against er_positive=0, her2_positive=1, pik3ca_mutation=1
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib effect in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt subgroup: +2.92 months (95% CI ~+2.85 to +3.00), p~0. Outside the subgroup: +0.0004, p=0.87. Effect is wholly contained in this 3-feature definition; HER2+ and PIK3CA-mut individually suppress benefit to zero.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for trastuzumab in HER2+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted trastuzumab effect in HER2+: -0.0002, p=0.95. No subgroup of HER2+ shows benefit.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for olaparib in BRCA-any subgroup; also BRCA1, BRCA2 separately
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib effect in BRCA-any: +0.025, p=0.75; BRCA1+: +0.084, p=0.46; BRCA2+: -0.033, p=0.78.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pembrolizumab in TNBC and TNBC/premenopausal subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted pembrolizumab effect in TNBC: +0.008, p=0.16. In TNBC/premenopausal: +0.015, p=0.05. Crude TNBC/premenopausal +0.275 attenuates ~95% with adjustment.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for sacituzumab in TNBC subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted sacituzumab effect in TNBC: +0.006, p=0.32.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for tamoxifen in ER+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted tamoxifen effect in ER+: ~0, NS in any postmenopausal/HER2- variant tested.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by approximately +2.9 months in the subgroup of patients with er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0; the effect is null (≈0 months) outside this subgroup, and her2_positive=1 and pik3ca_mutation=1 are the unfavorable values that nullify the palbociclib effect.

No other treatment in this dataset (treatment_tamoxifen, treatment_trastuzumab, treatment_olaparib, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, treatment_pembrolizumab) has a treatment-effect-defining subgroup with adjusted effect on pfs_months exceeding |0.05| months that survives multivariable adjustment and multiple-comparison scrutiny.

Within the palbociclib-responsive subgroup (er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0), the magnitude of palbociclib benefit is reduced as ki67_pct rises (continuous modifier); this is the only secondary modifier.

analyses.code: Final adjusted OLS within and outside ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib coefficient: +2.92 months inside ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt subgroup (n=18,681; p~0); +0.0004 months outside (n=31,319; p=0.87). The complete subgroup definition is er_positive=1 ∧ her2_positive=0 ∧ pik3ca_mutation=0; switching either her2_positive to 1 or pik3ca_mutation to 1 (or er_positive to 0) collapses the effect to zero.

analyses.code: Adjusted models for each non-palbociclib treatment in its canonical biomarker subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted effect estimates: tamoxifen(ER+) ≈0, trastuzumab(HER2+) -0.0002, olaparib(BRCAany) +0.025, sacituzumab(TNBC) +0.006, pembrolizumab(TNBC) +0.008. None reach |0.05| months; all p>0.15.
Palbociclib × Ki-67 interaction = -0.053 months per % Ki-67, p<1e-100. Within the responsive subgroup, the palbociclib gain shrinks as Ki-67 rises; no other meaningful modifier identified.
